summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing addressing old business cases including dismissal of two dumping complaints and resolutions for previously decided variance and conditional use cases. New business included granting a variance for an above-ground pool in a front yard due to topography challenges, and extensive testimony and public comment on a use variance request by Jeffrey Kelly to operate a truck repair business in a residential (R3) zoned area at 6823 Goshen Road, where the building was originally permitted as an agricultural-exempt structure.
decisions: Motion to approve minutes from previous meeting - passed
BZA 2021-003 dismissed (dumping case at 6942 Goshen Road resolved)
Resolution approving variance for David and Christina Tudor at 6866 Clubside Drive (deck in rear yard setback) - passed
Resolution approving conditional use for Alicia Reinerhart at 6790 Gaynor Road (56 ground-mounted solar panels) - passed
Resolution denying variance for Ryan Holmes at Codes of Barclay Woods on State Route 48 (1,366 sq ft home below 1,400 sq ft minimum) - passed
BZA 2022-010 variance approved for Anthony Moni at 6559 Smith Road for above-ground pool in front yard due to topography, with 10-foot side setback requirement maintained
action_items: Resolution for Tudor variance to be signed by chair and secretary
Resolution for Reinerhart conditional use to be signed by chair and secretary
Jeff Kelly use variance case (BZA 2022-013) deliberation to continue (transcript truncated before decision)
key_amounts: $3,300+ per year in taxes paid by Jeff Kelly on the commercial parcel
1,400 square feet minimum home size per zoning resolution (Ryan Holmes variance denial)
1,366 square feet proposed home by Ryan Holmes (denied)
4,800 square feet existing building at Jeff Kelly property
Commercial application fee charged to Jeff Kelly (twice the residential rate)
1,400 square feet of gravel parking lot at Kelly property
28 feet from Kelly building to adjacent property line (Pat Maynard's property)
148 feet from Kelly building to Maynard's house
100 feet required commercial building setback from adjacent property line per zoning resolution
notable_topics: Dismissal of two resolved dumping complaint cases from 2021
Approval of deck variance at 6866 Clubside Drive
Approval of solar panel conditional use at 6790 Gaynor Road
Denial of minimum square footage variance for Barclay Woods subdivision
Front yard pool variance granted at 6559 Smith Road due to topography
Jeff Kelly use variance request to operate truck repair business in R3 residential zone
Allegations that former zoning director Bob Seyfried approved Kelly's commercial operation verbally
Previously denied M2 zone change for Kelly property
EPA concerns about pressure washing contamination on Kelly property
Stop work order issued but alleged continued business operations
Agricultural exemption vs. commercial use discrepancy
Neighbor complaints about noise, traffic, diesel smoke, and safety concerns
Five letters of support from adjoining property owners
Whether use variance runs with the land or can be personal to applicant
attendees: Jeffrey Kelly (use variance applicant, J. Kelly Services owner)
Taylor Trout (attorney for Jeff Kelly, Keating Muting and Kleecamp)
Bob Seyfried (former Goshen Township zoning director, referenced)
David and Christina Tudor (6866 Clubside Drive, variance applicant)
Alicia Reinerhart (6790 Gaynor Road, solar panel applicant)
Ryan Holmes (Barclay Woods variance applicant, denied)
notes: This is Part 1 of 2 of the BZA meeting; the transcript is truncated before the Kelly use variance case concludes. Jeff Kelly's attorney Taylor Trout proposed that the use variance be personal to Kelly and not run with the land, but public commenter Lou Strower disputed this is legally possible under the zoning resolution. The case involves significant conflict between claims that former zoning director Bob Seyfried verbally approved the commercial use and the current zoning administrator's findings that the building was improperly permitted as agricultural-exempt. Multiple references to recorded trustee meetings where Trustee Dan Hodge allegedly said 'we're not going to shut you down.' The opponent Pat Maynard raised EPA contamination concerns from pressure washing truck engines without containment systems.
